verificationNote: Risk matrix constructed for OT/ICS environments based on IEC 62443-3-2 and NACSA Act 854 obligations. Band actions are indicative -- adjust thresholds and escalation paths to your organisation's risk appetite and regulatory context.
title: OT/ICS Risk Matrix (5x5)
description: Standard 5x5 risk matrix with OT-specific band definitions and actions. Impact is weighted toward safety and physical consequences. Band actions reference NACSA Act 854 notification obligations where applicable.

bands.action: Accept with documented rationale. Monitor during periodic risk review. No immediate action required.
bands.otNote: Even low-rated risks involving safety systems must be escalated for safety review regardless of score.

bands.action: Mitigate within 90 days. Implement compensating controls if primary mitigation requires outage window. Track in risk register with assigned owner.
bands.otNote: Medium risks on internet-facing or IT-connected OT assets should be prioritised for mitigation within 30 days due to higher exploitation speed.

bands.action: Mitigate within 30 days. Implement interim compensating controls immediately. Report to OT management and CISO. Include in next NACSA s21 risk assessment update.
bands.otNote: High risks with safety impact require safety review (HAZOP/SIL) and cannot be deferred beyond 30 days. Consider isolating affected zone pending mitigation.

bands.action: Immediate plant shutdown consideration and NACSA notification. Implement emergency controls within 24 hours. Board-level risk escalation required. Safety-critical risks at this level trigger mandatory process hazard analysis review.
bands.otNote: Critical risks may require controlled plant shutdown to prevent catastrophic outcome. NACSA Act 854 s26 requires notification within 6 hours if a cybersecurity incident is detected. Coordinate with operations engineering before any OT remediation action.

safetyOverride.description: Safety override rule: any risk with safety impact at Level 4 (Major) or Level 5 (Catastrophic) is automatically elevated to Critical band regardless of likelihood score. Safety risks cannot be accepted -- they must be mitigated or avoided.
safetyOverride.rationale: The potential for permanent disability, loss of life, or major environmental disaster cannot be offset by low likelihood. OT systems have demonstrated that low-probability safety events do occur (TRITON, Bhopal, Texas City).
